apps.name: Adobe Creative Cloud
apps.shortDescription: Adobe's desktop manager for installing Creative Cloud apps like Photoshop
apps.description: The Adobe Creative Cloud desktop app is Adobe's installer and license manager for its creative suite, used to install and update Photoshop, Illustrator, Premiere Pro, and 20+ other apps. Individual apps require a paid Creative Cloud subscription.

macos.note: Installer script requires admin privileges.

apps.name: Audacity
apps.shortDescription: Multi-track audio recording and editing
apps.description: Records and edits audio across multiple tracks, with effects, noise reduction, and export to common formats. Widely used for podcast recording, voiceovers, and cleaning up audio for video projects.

macos.note: Cask token 'audacity', HTTP 200 on formulae.brew.sh.

linux.note: ID exists on Flathub but the listing is not publisher-verified (community-maintained).

apps.name: balenaEtcher
apps.shortDescription: Flash OS images to USB drives and SD cards safely
apps.description: balenaEtcher is a cross-platform image flasher for writing OS images to USB drives and SD cards. It validates every byte written and protects against accidentally overwriting system hard drives.

macos.note: Cask version 2.1.6; installs balenaEtcher.app to /Applications, no sudo/pkg required.

apps.name: Bitwarden
apps.shortDescription: Open-source password manager and vault
apps.description: A password manager that stores logins, cards, and secure notes in an encrypted vault synced across devices. Open source, with free personal use and optional paid plans.

macos.note: Cask, version 2026.6.1. Note: the cask build lacks some features of the Mac App Store build (e.g. Safari extension integration).

linux.note: Verified-publisher listing by Bitwarden Inc. (verified via bitwarden.com).

apps.name: Brave
apps.shortDescription: Privacy-focused browser with built-in ad blocking
apps.description: A Chromium-based browser that blocks ads and trackers by default. Includes optional crypto wallet and rewards features, with Chrome extension compatibility.

apps.name: Claude Code CLI
apps.shortDescription: Anthropic's agentic coding assistant that lives in your terminal
apps.description: Claude Code is Anthropic's terminal-based coding agent: it reads your codebase, edits files, runs commands, and creates commits from natural-language prompts. Composable and scriptable in the Unix tradition, with MCP support for external tools.

apps.name: Claude Desktop
apps.shortDescription: Anthropic's desktop app whose Code tab is the Claude Code agentic coding...
apps.description: Anthropic ships a single Claude desktop app with three tabs — Chat, Cowork, and Code — where the Code tab is the full Claude Code experience: parallel sessions, integrated terminal/editor, diff review, live preview, and scheduled tasks. There is no separate 'Claude Code Desktop' binary; installing the Claude app gets you Claude Code on desktop (paid plan required for the Code tab).

windows.note: Claude Code is the Code tab of the Claude desktop app (subscription required).

macos.note: Claude Code is the Code tab of the Claude desktop app (subscription required).

apps.name: Codex CLI
apps.shortDescription: OpenAI's open-source coding agent that runs in your terminal
apps.description: Codex CLI is OpenAI's open-source (Apache-2.0, Rust) local coding agent that lets you write, edit, and understand code from the command line. It works with ChatGPT plans or an OpenAI API key.

apps.name: Codex Desktop
apps.shortDescription: OpenAI's Codex desktop app — a command center for running coding agents in...
apps.description: OpenAI's official Codex desktop app (macOS and Windows) is a command center for agentic software development: run multiple coding agents in parallel with built-in worktrees, cloud environments, diff review, skills, and scheduled automations. It picks up configuration from the Codex CLI and IDE extension.

windows.note: Microsoft Store listing; winget resolves it through its msstore source.

macos.note: v26.623.81905 (calendar-version scheme), installs Codex.app to /Applications without sudo, requires macOS 12+; ARM64 and x64 builds.

apps.name: Cursor
apps.shortDescription: AI-powered code editor built on VS Code
apps.description: Cursor is Anysphere's AI-native IDE built on the VS Code codebase, with integrated AI agents, chat, and predictive editing. It is proprietary software with free and paid subscription tiers.

macos.note: HTTP 200 on formulae.brew.sh; version 3.9.16, installs Cursor.app to /Applications without sudo and adds a 'cursor' CLI shim.

apps.name: Discord
apps.shortDescription: Voice, video, and text chat for communities
apps.description: Chat app built around servers and channels, with voice rooms, video calls, and screen sharing. Widely used by gaming groups, open-source projects, and online communities.

linux.note: Publisher-verified on Flathub (website verification via discordapp.com).

apps.name: Docker Desktop
apps.shortDescription: Build and run containers on your desktop
apps.description: Desktop application for building, running, and managing Docker containers, with a GUI dashboard, Kubernetes support, and the Docker CLI included. Free for personal use and small businesses; larger companies need a paid subscription.

macos.note: Correction: current cask token is docker-desktop (v4.80.0); the old token 'docker' is listed in old_tokens and now refers to the CLI formula space.

apps.name: Elgato Stream Deck
apps.shortDescription: Configuration software for Elgato Stream Deck macro keypads
apps.description: Elgato Stream Deck is the official companion software for Stream Deck hardware, letting you assign actions, plugins, and profiles to customizable LCD keys for streaming and productivity workflows.

macos.note: .pkg installer; expect an admin-privilege prompt.

apps.name: Epic Games Launcher
apps.shortDescription: Epic Games Store client and game launcher
apps.description: Official client for the Epic Games Store. Claim the weekly free games, install and update your library, and launch titles like Fortnite and Unreal Engine tools.

macos.note: Cask; verified via formulae.brew.sh API.

apps.name: Firefox
apps.shortDescription: Open-source browser from Mozilla
apps.description: Mozilla's free and open-source web browser, focused on privacy and standards compliance. The main independent alternative to Chromium-based browsers.

apps.name: GameSir Connect
apps.shortDescription: Official companion software for GameSir game controllers
apps.description: GameSir's companion app for controllers such as the Cyclone 2 and T3 Pro: firmware updates, button remapping, and stick/trigger calibration. Published by Guangzhou Chicken Run Network Technology Co., Ltd (GameSir).

windows.note: Also on the Microsoft Store as XPFMNZ2F440G2L if this id ever rots.

apps.name: Git
apps.shortDescription: Version control system for tracking code changes
apps.description: The standard distributed version control system. Tracks changes to files, lets multiple people work on the same codebase, and powers services like GitHub and GitLab. On Windows this is Git for Windows, which includes Git Bash.

macos.note: Homebrew formula (CLI tool), not a cask.

apps.name: Google Chrome
apps.shortDescription: Google's fast, widely used web browser
apps.description: The world's most widely used web browser. Syncs bookmarks, passwords, and tabs across devices with a Google account, and supports a large extension ecosystem.

linux.note: Flathub listing exists but is a community-maintained wrapper, not verified by or affiliated with Google.

apps.name: HandBrake
apps.shortDescription: Converts video files to modern formats
apps.description: Converts video from nearly any format into widely supported codecs like H.264, H.265, and AV1. Includes device presets, batch queueing, subtitle handling, and DVD/Blu-ray ripping from unencrypted sources.

macos.note: Corrected: cask was renamed from 'handbrake' to 'handbrake-app' (old token aliased). A separate 'handbrake' formula provides only HandBrakeCLI.

linux.note: Flathub listing verified by publisher (website handbrake.fr).

apps.name: Hermes Agent CLI
apps.shortDescription: Nous Research's self-improving AI agent for the terminal, with a closed...
apps.description: Hermes Agent is Nous Research's open-source (MIT) self-improving AI agent: it creates skills from experience, improves them during use, and runs locally, on a VPS, or serverless — reachable from the terminal TUI or messaging platforms (Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, Signal). Model-agnostic across Nous Portal, OpenRouter, OpenAI, and any compatible endpoint.

apps.name: LM Studio
apps.shortDescription: Desktop app to discover, download, and run local LLMs privately
apps.description: LM Studio (Element Labs) is a cross-platform desktop app for discovering, downloading, and running open-weight LLMs locally with GPU acceleration, a chat UI, and an OpenAI-compatible local server. Free for personal use.

macos.note: v0.4.18,1 — installs 'LM Studio.app' to /Applications without sudo; in step with winget.

linux.note: Community Flathub packaging of the official Linux build.

apps.name: Medal.tv
apps.shortDescription: Game clip recorder and sharing platform
apps.description: Medal.tv records and clips gameplay in the background without dropping frames, with built-in editing and one-click sharing to Medal and other social platforms. Published by Medal B.V.

windows.note: Stub installer that pulls the latest Medal build and self-updates.

apps.name: MobaXterm
apps.shortDescription: All-in-one Windows terminal with SSH, X11 server, RDP, and SFTP
apps.description: MobaXterm is a Windows remote-computing toolbox combining SSH, RDP, VNC, SFTP, Telnet, and an integrated X11 server with a tabbed terminal and Unix commands. The Home Edition is free for personal and professional use.

apps.name: MultiMC
apps.shortDescription: Minecraft launcher for managing multiple isolated instances
apps.description: MultiMC is an alternative Minecraft launcher that keeps multiple cleanly separated game instances — each with its own mods, resource packs, and settings — with fine-grained Java control and modpack import from Modrinth, CurseForge, Technic, and more.

macos.note: Cask is deprecated (fails Gatekeeper) and will be disabled 2026-09-01; prefer Prism Launcher on macOS.

apps.name: Notepad++
apps.shortDescription: Free open-source text and source code editor for Windows
apps.description: Notepad++ is a free, GPL-licensed source code editor and Notepad replacement for Windows, supporting syntax highlighting for dozens of languages with a lightweight footprint.

apps.name: Notion
apps.shortDescription: All-in-one workspace for notes, docs, and tasks
apps.description: A connected workspace for notes, documents, wikis, project tracking, and databases. The desktop app syncs with Notion's cloud service and supports team collaboration.

macos.note: Cask, version 7.24.0.

apps.name: NVIDIA App
apps.shortDescription: NVIDIA's unified companion app for GeForce drivers, settings, and recording
apps.description: The NVIDIA App is the official companion application for GeForce GPUs, unifying driver updates, game optimization, GPU settings, and ShadowPlay capture. It replaced GeForce Experience as NVIDIA's current desktop software starting in late 2024.

windows.note: Microsoft Store listing; winget resolves it through its msstore source.

apps.name: OBS Studio
apps.shortDescription: Free screen recording and live streaming
apps.description: Records your screen, webcam, and audio, and streams live to platforms like Twitch and YouTube. The standard tool for screen capture and streaming, with scenes, sources, and audio mixing built in.

macos.note: Cask token 'obs', HTTP 200 on formulae.brew.sh.

linux.note: Flathub listing verified by publisher (website obsproject.com).

apps.name: Obsidian
apps.shortDescription: Note-taking app built on local Markdown files
apps.description: A knowledge base and note-taking app that works on top of a local folder of plain-text Markdown files. Supports linking notes together, a graph view, and a large plugin ecosystem, with your data staying on your own machine.

macos.note: Cask, version 1.12.7.

linux.note: Verified-publisher listing (verified via obsidian.md), though Obsidian marks the Flatpak distribution as unsupported.

apps.name: Opera GX
apps.shortDescription: Gaming-focused web browser with CPU/RAM/network limiters
apps.description: Opera GX is a Chromium-based browser built for gamers, adding GX Control resource limiters, Twitch/Discord sidebar integrations, and heavy theming on top of Opera's privacy and efficiency features.

macos.note: Cask confirmed via formulae.brew.sh API (HTTP 200). Version 132.0.5905.110; installs 'Opera GX.app' to /Applications without sudo.

apps.name: Oracle JDK
apps.shortDescription: Oracle's official Java SE Development Kit, current release (JDK 26)
apps.description: The Oracle JDK is Oracle's official build of the Java SE Development Kit for building and running Java applications. As of July 2026 the newest release line is JDK 26 (26.0.1), distributed under the Oracle No-Fee Terms and Conditions.

windows.note: Per-major-version id: bump to Oracle.JDK.<n> each March/September JDK release.

macos.note: HTTP 200; version 26.0.1. Installs via .pkg installer requiring admin/sudo (pkgutil id com.oracle.jdk-26.0.1), not a drag-to-/Applications app. Cask token is version-less and tracks the latest release automatically.

apps.name: Prism Launcher
apps.shortDescription: Open-source Minecraft launcher with instances
apps.description: A free, open-source Minecraft launcher that manages multiple isolated game instances, mod loaders, and modpacks side by side. Community fork of MultiMC.

macos.note: Cask; verified via formulae.brew.sh API.

apps.name: Python 3
apps.shortDescription: Popular programming language and interpreter
apps.description: The Python programming language interpreter and standard library. Widely used for scripting, automation, data science, and web development. Installs the current stable 3.14 series.

windows.note: Correction from candidate Python.Python.3.13: winget minor-version folders under Python.Python.3 go up to 14, matching current stable 3.14.

macos.note: Homebrew formula, not a cask. The bare 'python' name is only an alias (API endpoint for it 404s); 'python' and 'python3' both resolve to python@3.14 (3.14.6).

apps.name: RustDesk
apps.shortDescription: Open-source remote desktop with optional self-hosted relay
apps.description: RustDesk is an open-source remote desktop and screen-sharing application, positioned as a TeamViewer alternative. It works out of the box with public rendezvous servers or a fully self-hosted relay.

macos.note: Cask version 1.4.8; installs RustDesk.app to /Applications, no sudo. Requires macOS 12+; Apple Silicon and Intel builds.

apps.name: Signal
apps.shortDescription: Private, end-to-end encrypted messaging
apps.description: Messaging app with end-to-end encryption on by default for texts, calls, and file sharing. Run by a nonprofit; the desktop app links to Signal on your phone.

linux.note: Community-maintained Flatpak; not officially endorsed or maintained by Signal Foundation. Signal officially distributes a Debian apt repo instead.

apps.name: Slack
apps.shortDescription: Team messaging organized into channels
apps.description: Workplace messaging app with channels, threads, huddles, and file sharing. The standard team chat tool at many companies, with a large ecosystem of integrations.

linux.note: Community-maintained wrapper; Flathub listing states it is not verified by or affiliated with Slack Technologies. Slack for Linux is beta.

apps.name: Spotify
apps.shortDescription: Music and podcast streaming service
apps.description: Spotify's desktop client for streaming millions of tracks, podcasts, and playlists, with cross-device sync and personalized recommendations. Published by Spotify AB.

macos.note: Cask confirmed via formulae.brew.sh API (HTTP 200). Version 1.2.92.148; installs Spotify.app to /Applications without sudo.

linux.note: Community-maintained repackage of Spotify’s Linux client.

apps.name: Steam
apps.shortDescription: The biggest PC game store and library
apps.description: Valve's game store and launcher. Buy, download, and play PC games, keep them updated automatically, and sync saves and friends lists across machines.

windows.note: Confirmed at manifests/v/Valve/Steam in microsoft/winget-pkgs. Single version folder because Steam self-updates after install.

macos.note: Cask; verified via formulae.brew.sh API.

linux.note: ID confirmed to exist on Flathub, but the listing is community-maintained and not verified by Valve (verification field is null).

apps.name: Telegram
apps.shortDescription: Official desktop client for the Telegram messenger
apps.description: Telegram Desktop is the official cross-platform desktop client for the Telegram messaging service, focused on speed and security with cloud-synced chats and unlimited media sharing.

macos.note: HTTP 200; version 6.9.3, installs 'Telegram Desktop.app' to /Applications without sudo. This is the Qt tdesktop client matching the product identity; a different cask 'telegram' ships Telegram's native Swift macOS app.

apps.name: Termius
apps.shortDescription: Cross-platform SSH client with sync, SFTP, and port forwarding
apps.description: Termius is a modern SSH and SFTP client with encrypted cross-device sync, snippets, port forwarding, and team sharing. It runs on desktop and mobile with a freemium subscription model.

macos.note: Cask version 9.40.1; installs Termius.app to /Applications, no sudo. sha256 is :no_check (vendor serves an unversioned auto-update URL) — mild rot/pinning caveat.

linux.note: Community Flathub wrapper; vendor’s own Linux channel is Snap.

apps.name: Visual Studio Code
apps.shortDescription: Microsoft's free code editor with extensions
apps.description: A free code editor from Microsoft with built-in Git support, debugging, and a large extension marketplace. The most widely used editor for general-purpose programming and web development.

linux.note: Exists on Flathub but is a community-maintained repackaging of Microsoft's proprietary build, explicitly not supported by Microsoft; Flathub verification field is null.

apps.name: VLC
apps.shortDescription: Plays almost any video or audio file
apps.description: A media player that handles nearly every video and audio format without extra codecs, plus DVDs, audio CDs, and network streams. It can also convert files and record your desktop or streams.

macos.note: Cask token 'vlc', HTTP 200 on formulae.brew.sh.

linux.note: ID exists on Flathub but the listing is not publisher-verified (community-maintained).

apps.name: Willow Voice
apps.shortDescription: AI voice typing that works in every app
apps.description: Willow is a system-wide AI dictation tool: speak and it types into any Windows application — email, chat, code, or docs. Published by Willow Care Inc.

windows.note: Microsoft Store listing; winget resolves it through its msstore source.
